ModuleName: 4- The Stack: Structure and Use Cases

Artifacts.Name: Module Topics
Artifacts.Description: Covers stack data structure concepts including the LIFO principle, core operations (push, pop), internal implementation details, and hands-on JavaScript class construction, serving as the navigational outline that guides learners through the module's progression of topics.

Artifacts.Name: Module Topics (HTML)
Artifacts.Description: Outlines the module's topics on the stack data structure, covering what a stack is, its LIFO organization, core operations, and use cases, giving learners and instructors a structured overview of what will be taught before engaging with detailed materials.

Artifacts.Name: Undo Mechanisms and Other Real-World Use Cases
Artifacts.Description: A reading that explores practical, everyday applications of stacks — including undo functionality in text editors and browser navigation history — helping learners connect the abstract LIFO concept to real software they already use.

Artifacts.Name: Introduction to the Stack Data Structure
Artifacts.Description: An introductory reading that defines the stack data structure, explains its restricted top-only access model, and establishes foundational vocabulary such as push and pop for learners encountering stacks for the first time.

Artifacts.Name: The LIFO Principle
Artifacts.Description: A focused reading that explains the Last-In, First-Out (LIFO) principle in depth, clarifying how this governing rule shapes stack behavior and distinguishes stacks from other data structures.

Artifacts.Name: Internal Implementation of a Stack
Artifacts.Description: A reading that covers the internal mechanics of stack implementation, including how memory is organized, how elements are tracked, and the trade-offs between array-based and linked-list-based approaches, giving learners a under-the-hood understanding of the structure.

Artifacts.Name: Building a Stack in JavaScript
Artifacts.Description: A hands-on reading and coding guide that walks learners through implementing a stack class in JavaScript, demonstrating push, pop, and related operations with a practical, language-specific example.

Artifacts.Name: Expression Evaluation Using Stacks
Artifacts.Description: A reading that explains how stacks are used to evaluate mathematical expressions and handle nested parentheses, walking learners through the algorithmic process of using stacks to parse and resolve expressions correctly.

Artifacts.Name: Undo Mechanisms and Other Real-World Use Cases
Artifacts.Description: A reading that explores practical, everyday applications of stacks — including undo functionality in text editors and browser navigation history — helping learners connect the abstract LIFO concept to real software they already use.

Artifacts.Name: Cloze Passage (Interactive)
Artifacts.Description: A fill-in-the-blank reading activity covering key terminology and concepts of the stack data structure, where learners type missing terms into a passage and receive instant right/wrong feedback to reinforce their understanding of stack vocabulary and principles.
